requirement: Phase 0.5 specs: platform architecture, tenant context, schema renames, CLAUDE.md update per GarudaX_Strategy_Directive.md

tasks.title: Platform architecture spec
tasks.description:
Write the comprehensive multi-tenant platform architecture spec at docs/platform-architecture.md.

This is the foundational document for the GarudaX multi-tenant pivot. Read GarudaX_Strategy_Directive.md in full — every section must be addressed.

Cover these sections:

1. **Overview** — GarudaX is the platform, tenants are venues. ace-commodities (live) and mse-equities (onboarding) are the two tenants.

2. **Tenant Model** — platform.tenants table: id (slug like 'ace-commodities'), name, status (ACTIVE/SUSPENDED/ONBOARDING), flagship boolean, governance_tier, onboarding_metadata JSONB, created_at, updated_at. Tenant ID is a first-class construct per §2.1.

3. **Data Isolation** — Per-tenant Postgres schemas: ace_reference, ace_participants, ace_exchange, ace_clearing, ace_compliance, ace_warehouse, ace_market_data, ace_securities + mse_* equivalents. Platform schemas: platform.tenants, platform.audit. auth stays platform-level. Cross-tenant queries require platform-admin role per §2.2.

4. **Tenant Context Propagation** — HTTP: X-Tenant-ID signed header (HMAC-SHA256 with platform secret). gRPC: tenant_id metadata key. Kafka: topic convention {tenant_id}.{domain}.{event}. Middleware rejects any request without valid tenant context. Context stored in Go context.Context and passed to all store/engine calls.

5. **Identity & Auth** — auth schema stays platform-level. JWT claims include tenant_roles: {"ace-commodities": ["admin"], "mse-equities": ["trader"]}. Session tokens are scoped per tenant. IRSA roles: garudax-{tenant_id}-{service}. KMS CMKs per tenant.

6. **Platform Control Plane** — Tenant registry service (CRUD on tenants). Lifecycle workflows: provision tenant → create schemas → create Kafka topics → create IAM roles → create dashboards. Platform-admin API at /platform/v1/* (separate from tenant APIs at /api/v1/*). Platform-admin UI (separate React SPA or admin-ui tab).

7. **Operational Isolation** — One tenant can halt/rollback without affecting another. Kafka consumer groups per tenant. Redis keyspace per tenant ({tenant_id}:*). Circuit breakers per tenant. Health checks per tenant.

8. **Governance Isolation** — Trading calendar, listing rules, circuit breaker thresholds, KYC policies, position limits — all per-tenant config. Platform-wide config is separate with platform-governance workflow.

9. **Agent Fabric Tenancy** — MCP tools carry tenant_id. Bot invocations scoped per tenant. Post-mortems scoped per tenant. Cross-tenant analysis is explicit platform-admin operation.

10. **MSE Flagship Requirements** — T+1/T+2 settlement profiles, corporate actions service, opening/closing auctions, short selling rules, FRC reporting, MCSD integration. When design conflicts arise, MSE wins per §2.3.

11. **Migration Strategy** — Phase 0.5 (this): specs. Phase 0.6: ace-commodities retrofit (schema renames, topic renames, code updates). Phase 0.7: mse-equities build. Retrofit before MSE per §5.

12. **Appendix: Schema Rename Map** — reference→ace_reference, participants→ace_participants, exchange→ace_exchange, clearing→ace_clearing, compliance→ace_compliance, warehouse→ace_warehouse, market_data→ace_market_data, securities→ace_securities. auth stays as-is (platform-level).

Be SPECIFIC — exact table names, header formats, JWT claim structures, Kafka topic examples, middleware pseudo-code. This spec will drive all implementation tasks.

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md

tasks.title: Schema rename migration plan
Update CLAUDE.md per directive §7
tasks.description:
Update CLAUDE.md with the multi-tenancy pivot per GarudaX_Strategy_Directive.md §7.

Read GarudaX_Strategy_Directive.md §7 for exact requirements.
Read the current CLAUDE.md to understand what needs updating.
Read docs/platform-architecture.md (from T1) for the architecture decisions.

Changes to make:

1. **Product Vision section** (top of file) — Rewrite to reflect GarudaX as the platform:
   - Product is GarudaX — multi-tenant, AI-native operating platform for regulated trading venues
   - ace-commodities is one tenant (live)
   - mse-equities is the flagship tenant (onboarding)
   - The commodity exchange features are tenant-specific, not platform features

2. **Current State** — Update to reflect:
   - 10+ Go services (now tenant-aware)
   - Securities module built (Phase 7 complete)
   - Multi-tenant pivot in progress (Phase 0.5)

3. **Roadmap** — Replace old phases with:
   - Phase 0.5: Multi-tenant platform specs (this sprint)
   - Phase 0.6: ace-commodities retrofit (schema renames, tenant context)
   - Phase 0.7: mse-equities flagship build
   - Phase 8+: FIX gateway, bot expansion (now tenant-aware)

4. **Learned Patterns section** — Append new pattern:
   ```
   ### Pattern: Multi-tenancy directive (2026-04-23)
   - Every task from directive-2026-04 forward assumes multi-tenancy
   - Tenant ID is a first-class argument to every domain operation
   - No service accepts traffic with an unresolved tenant
   - Reviewer rejects any task without tenant context in its API surface
   - ace-commodities retrofit must complete before mse-equities build
   ```

5. **File Layout Convention** — Add venues/ directory:
   ```
   project-root/
     venues/
       ace-commodities/   ← commodity exchange tenant config
       mse-equities/      ← MSE flagship tenant config
   ```

Do NOT rewrite the entire CLAUDE.md — only update the specific sections listed above. Preserve all other content (Softhouse architecture, agent roles, state management, etc.).

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T3.md
